Chapter 4 — Conducting, Not Prompting: T — The conducting framework is Simon applied to a new instrument. You are bounded. You cannot hold an entire build in your

beats.text: James Clerk Maxwell American polymath; Nobel laureate in economics (1978), Turing Award (1975). Simon's most consequential idea is bounded rationality: the human decision-maker does not optimize across all possibilities because optimization across all possibilities exceeds cognitive capacity. Instead, the human satisfices , chooses a path that is good enough against criteria the human can hold in mind at once (Simon, 1957, Models of Man; Simon, Administrative Behavior, 1947). Simon's claim was not that humans are stupid.
